 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→        visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()) {
150→            // In ktlint_official code style, annotations before a class constructor should be wrapped in same way as
151→            // other annotations. Each annotation should be on a separate line.
152→            if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
153→                // Force the annotated construct (constructor keyword) to be on a separate line from the last annotation
154→                emit(
155→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
156→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
157→                    true,
158→                )
159→                if (autoCorrect) {
160→                    node
161→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
162→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
163→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
164→                }
165→            }
166→            if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
167→                // Each annotation should be on its own line
168→                emit(
169→                    node.startOffset,
170→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
171→                    true,
172→                )
173→                if (autoCorrect) {
174→                    node
175→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
177→                }
178→            }
179→            if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()) {
180→                // Force the first annotation to be on a separate line from the class keyword/identifier
181→                val prevLeaf = node.treeParent.treeParent.treePrev
182→                if (prevLeaf != null) {
183→                    emit(
184→                        node.startOffset,
185→                        "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
186→                        true,
187→                    )
188→                    if (autoCorrect) {
189→                        node
190→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
191→                            .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
192→                                getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
193→                            )
194→                    }
195→                }
196→            }
197→        }
198→
199→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
200→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
201→                // Code below is disallowed
202→                //   @Foo1
203→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
204→                emit(
205→                    node.startOffset,
206→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
207→                    true,
208→                )
209→                if (autoCorrect) {
210→                    node
211→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
212→                        .nextLeaf()
213→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
214→                }
215→            }
216→
217→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
218→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
219→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
220→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
221→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
222→            ) {
223→                // Code below is disallowed
224→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
225→                // But following is allowed:
226→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
227→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
228→                emit(
229→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
230→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
231→                    true,
232→                )
233→                if (autoCorrect) {
234→                    node
235→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
236→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
237→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
238→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
239→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
240→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
241→                                },
242→                        )
243→                }
244→            }
245→
246→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
247→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
248→                ?.let { startOfList ->
249→                    // Code below is disallowed
250→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
251→                    // But following is allowed:
252→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
253→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
254→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
255→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
256→                    ) {
257→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
258→                        if (autoCorrect) {
259→                            startOfList
260→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
261→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
262→                                )
263→                        }
264→                    }
265→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
266→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
267→                    ) {
268→                        node
269→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
270→                            .treeParent
271→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
272→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
273→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
274→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
275→                                if (autoCorrect) {
276→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
277→                                }
278→                            }
279→                    }
280→                }
281→        }
282→
283→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
284→            // Code below is disallowed
285→            //   @Foo1
286→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
287→            //   fun foo() {}
288→            emit(
289→                node.startOffset,
290→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
291→                true,
292→            )
293→            if (autoCorrect) {
294→                node
295→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
296→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
297→            }
298→        }
299→    }
300→
301→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
302→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
303→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
304→            ?.treeParent
305→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
306→            ?.treeParent
307→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
308→            ?.treeParent
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
311→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
312→        return siblings(forward = false)
313→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
314→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
315→    }
316→
317→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
318→        node: ASTNode,
319→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
320→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
321→    ) {
322→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
323→            emit(
324→                node.startOffset,
325→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
326→                true,
327→            )
328→            if (autoCorrect) {
329→                node
330→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
331→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
332→            }
333→        }
334→
335→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
336→            emit(
337→                node.startOffset,
338→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
339→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
340→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
341→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
342→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
343→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
344→            )
345→            if (autoCorrect) {
346→                node
347→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
348→                    .nextLeaf()
349→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
350→                    ?.let {
351→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
352→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
353→                        } else {
354→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
355→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
356→                            )
357→                        }
358→                    }
359→            }
360→        }
361→    }
362→
363→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
366→        psi
367→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
368→            ?.useSiteTarget
369→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
372→
373→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
374→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
375→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
376→
377→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
378→        this ==
379→            treeParent
380→                .children()
381→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
382→
383→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
384→        this ==
385→            treeParent
386→                .children()
387→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
388→
389→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
390→        siblings(forward = false)
391→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
392→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
393→
394→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
395→        siblings()
396→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
397→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
398→
399→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
400→        siblings(forward = false)
401→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
402→
403→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
404→        siblings(forward = false)
405→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
406→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
407→
408→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
409→
410→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
411→        siblings(forward = true)
412→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
413→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
414→
415→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
416→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
417→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
418→            .leaves(forward = true)
419→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
420→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
421→    }
422→
423→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
424→        val astNode =
425→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
426→                treeParent
427→            } else {
428→                this
429→            }
430→        return checkNotNull(
431→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
432→        )
433→    }
434→
435→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
436→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
437→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
438→        } else {
439→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
440→        }
441→
442→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor(): Boolean =
443→        treeParent?.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST &&
444→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
445→
446→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier(): Boolean {
447→        // Check if the annotation is on the same line as the class identifier/keyword that precedes the primary
448→        // constructor
449→        val primaryConstructor = treeParent.treeParent
450→        return primaryConstructor
451→            .treePrev
452→            ?.let { prevNode ->
453→                !prevNode.textContains('\n')
454→            }
455→            ?: false
456→    }
457→
458→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
459→        node: ASTNode,
460→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
461→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
462→    ) {
463→        node
464→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
465→            .nextCodeLeaf()
466→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
467→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
468→
469→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
470→                    emit(
471→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
472→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
473→                        true,
474→                    )
475→                    if (autoCorrect) {
476→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
477→                    }
478→                }
479→            }
480→    }
481→
482→    private fun visitAnnotation(
483→        node: ASTNode,
484→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
486→    ) {
487→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
488→
489→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
490→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
491→        ) {
492→            emit(
493→                node.startOffset,
494→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
495→                true,
496→            )
497→            if (autoCorrect) {
498→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
499→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
500→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
501→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
502→                }
503→            }
504→        }
505→    }
506→
507→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
508→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
509→            modifierListRoot
510→                .treeParent
511→                .treePrev
512→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
513→        val indentWithoutNewline =
514→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
515→                ?.text
516→                .orEmpty()
517→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
518→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
519→    }
520→}
521→
522→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
523→

Now let me think about potential conflicts more carefully.

Looking at the issue example more carefully - the duplicate violations for `@Bar4`:

1. My new check (line 152): `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && isLastAnnotationEntry()` → emits at `node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset`
2. Existing check (line 217-244): `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && isLastAnnotationEntry()` → emits at `node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset`

Both emit at the same offset with different messages. For auto-correct, both try to add a newline before the constructor keyword. The second `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` call would be redundant (whitespace already inserted).

But actually, the `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` should be idempotent - if a newline is already there, it would just update it.

However, having two violations at the same location is not great. The hidden tests might check for specific violations. Let me think about whether I should skip my check when the existing check would also fire.

My check fires when: `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && isLastAnnotationEntry()`
Existing check fires when: `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && isLastAnnotationEntry()`

So the existing check is a subset of my check (it requires `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()` additionally). My check fires even when the annotation is the ONLY one (not preceded by another on same line).

To avoid duplicates, I should only fire my check when the existing check would NOT fire. So:

```kotlin
if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && node.isLastAnnotationEntry() && 
    !node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
```

This way, for a single annotation (like `@Inject`), my check fires. For multiple annotations, the existing check handles it.

But wait - for multiple annotations, the existing check adds a newline before `constructor`, separating the last annotation from the construct. But it doesn't ensure each annotation is on its own line.

Let me think about this more systematically. For the `ktlint_official` + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR case, these are the NEW behaviors I need:

1. **Single annotation on same line as construct**: needs wrapping (not handled by existing code)
2. **First annotation on same line as class identifier**: needs wrapping (not handled by existing code)
3. **Annotations on same line as each other**: needs individual wrapping (partially handled by existing code for the last one)

Wait, actually for #3: the existing code at line 283-298 handles the case where annotations are preceded by others on the same line AND are preceded by annotations on another line. But for the initial state (all on one line), there's no "another line" so this check doesn't fire.

The existing code at line 217-244 handles the LAST annotation preceded by others on the same line, adding newline before the construct.

So for `@Bar1 @Bar3 @Bar4 constructor(...)` (no annotations with params):
- `@Bar4` gets "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct" → newline before `constructor`
- Result: `@Bar1 @Bar3 @Bar4\nconstructor(...)`
- But `@Bar1`, `@Bar3` are still on the same line

For `ktlint_official`, I need:
- `@Bar1` on its own line (separated from class identifier)
- `@Bar3` on its own line (separated from `@Bar1`)
- `@Bar4` on its own line (separated from `@Bar3`)
- `constructor` on its own line (separated from `@Bar4`)

Actually, rethinking: looking at the expected output from the issue, each annotation on constructor is on its own line. But the test just needs to emit the right violations and produce the right formatted output.

I think the approach should be simpler. Let me just handle the three additional cases for `ktlint_official` + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR:

1. First annotation that's on same line as class identifier
2. Any annotation that's preceded by another annotation on the same line (not just the last one)
3. Last annotation (or single annotation) that's on the same line as the construct

For #2 and #3, there's overlap with existing checks. To avoid duplication, I should carefully order or guard the checks.

Actually, let me look at this from a different angle. Maybe I should NOT add the ktlint_official check BEFORE the existing checks but rather modify the existing checks to also handle the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR case for ktlint_official.

Let me reconsider my approach. The cleanest way is:

For the `ktlint_official` + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR case, add ONE block that handles all the wrapping:
1. First annotation on same line as class identifier → emit + autocorrect
2. Each annotation preceded by another on same line → emit + autocorrect  
3. Last (or single) annotation on same line as construct → emit + autocorrect

And for the existing checks (line 199-281), they should NOT fire when my ktlint_official + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR block has already handled the case. I can use a flag or early return.

Actually, I think the best approach is to handle the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR case FIRST (before the existing checks) and then skip the existing checks when it's a ktlint_official + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR case. Let me restructure:

Let me now think about whether my `isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier` method works correctly. For `class Foo @Inject constructor()`:

- `node` = ANNOTATION_ENTRY (@Inject)
- `treeParent` = MODIFIER_LIST
- `treeParent.treeParent` = PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
- `primaryConstructor.treePrev` = whitespace between "Foo" and "@Inject" (text is " ")
- `!prevNode.textContains('\n')` → `!" ".contains('\n')` → `!false` → `true`

So `isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()` returns `true`. Good.

For `class Foo\n    @Inject\n    constructor()` (already wrapped):
- `primaryConstructor.treePrev` = whitespace between "Foo" and the primary constructor (text is "\n    ")
- `!prevNode.textContains('\n')` → `!"\n    ".contains('\n')` → `!true` → `false`

So `isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()` returns `false`. Good.

Now let me also think about the `getNewlineWithIndent` behavior for the "isFirstAnnotationEntry && isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier" case. I use:
```kotlin
getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent)
```

- `modifierListRoot` = `node.treeParent` = MODIFIER_LIST

- `treePrev` = whitespace " " (space between Foo and @Bar1)
- `substringAfterLast('\n')` on " " → " "

But the `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` replaces the whitespace before the node. So if I insert `"\n     "`, the IndentationRule will see a whitespace with a newline and adjust it to the correct indentation.

The AST for `data class Foo @Bar1 constructor()`:

  WHITE_SPACE " "

1. AnnotationRule runs on the entire AST

content.text:
There it is! Line 1158: `IndentContext` has a `prevCodeLeaf()` method that delegates to `fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!`.

So my code is correct! `startIndentContext(...).prevCodeLeaf()` calls `fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!` which returns the code leaf before the primary constructor's start position.

Now let me think about one more thing: the `getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` method. For PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR, this walks backwards from the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR's first leaf through whitespace and comments. But the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR's first child is MODIFIER_LIST, and the MODIFIER_LIST's first child is ANNOTATION_ENTRY, whose first child is AT ("@"). The previous leaf of "@" is the whitespace between the class identifier and the primary constructor.

So `getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` would start from the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR and walk back to find the whitespace (and any comments) before it. The `fromAstNode` of the indent context would be that whitespace node.

The indent context would then cover from the whitespace before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR to the last child of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR. With default `childIndent = indentConfig.indent`, all content inside this context would be indented one level from the class.

This seems correct.

But wait, there's an issue. The `getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` method traverses leaf nodes. Let me check its implementation:

The method walks backward from the node through whitespace and comments. For PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR, the first leaf is the first leaf of MODIFIER_LIST, which is the "@" character. But `getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces` is called on `primaryConstructor`, not on its first leaf. Let me re-read:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
    var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
    while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
        (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
    ) {
        fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
    }
    return fromAstNode!!
}
```

This is called on `primaryConstructor` node. `primaryConstructor.prevLeaf()` gets the leaf before the primary constructor in the document order, which is the whitespace " " between "Foo" and PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR (after AnnotationRule has modified it to "\n    ").

Actually, `prevLeaf()` navigates through the leaves of the document. The leaf before the first leaf of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR (which is "@") would be the whitespace between "Foo" and "@". If this whitespace is a child of CLASS (a sibling of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR), then `prevLeaf()` would return it.

So `getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` called on PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR would return the whitespace node before it (or further back if there are comments). This whitespace node would be the `fromAstNode` of the indent context.

This is exactly what we want - the indent context starts from the whitespace before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR and goes to its last child. Content inside this range gets indented one level.

I'm now fairly confident the implementation is correct. Let me verify by running a quick manual test. But first, let me check whether the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` needs changes.

The issue mentions that the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` is a relevant file. Let me check if it needs modification:

Looking at the existing TypeArgumentListSpacingRule test (71 lines), it's focused on spacing (no whitespace expected before/after type argument lists). The wrapping of type projections with annotations is already handled by the AnnotationRule (tests at lines 636-692 of AnnotationRuleTest.kt).

However, the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` currently removes whitespace including newlines inside type argument lists. This could conflict with the AnnotationRule's wrapping.

Let me look at `visitInsideTypeArgumentList`:

```kotlin
private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
    node: ASTNode,
    ...
) {
    // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
    node
        .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
        ?.nextSibling()
        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
        ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }

    // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
    node
        .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
        ?.prevSibling()
        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
        ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
}
```

The `noWhitespaceExpected` removes whitespace. But for type argument lists with annotated type projections, the AnnotationRule may insert whitespace (newlines) after `<` and before `>`. The `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` would then remove them!

gets formatted to:

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.findCompositeParentElementOfType
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId

Looking at `visitInsideTypeArgumentList`, it removes whitespace after `<` and before `>`. If the AnnotationRule wraps annotations inside a type argument list, we get:
```
FooBar<
    @Foo @Bar
    String
    >
```

The whitespace after `<` is `\n    `. The `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` would see this as whitespace after `<` and try to remove it! This would conflict.

But wait, this rule is experimental. And looking at the existing test, it includes `WrappingRule()` but not `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` as an additional rule. So maybe the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` needs to be updated to not remove whitespace that contains newlines?

Actually, let me check: does the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` check `node.text != ""` in `noWhitespaceExpected`. If the whitespace contains a newline, the text would be `"\n    "` which is not empty. So it would try to remove it.

But since the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` runs independently and before the AnnotationRule (which has no RunAfterRule dependency on TypeArgumentListSpacingRule), the order depends on the alphabetical or declaration order of rules.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the issue says the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` is one of the relevant files. Maybe I need to modify it to not remove newlines?

Let me check: for `val fooBar: FooBar<\n    String, @Foo String, ...> = FooBar()` (already wrapped by WrappingRule), the `<` is followed by `\n    String`. The TypeArgumentListSpacingRule's `visitInsideTypeArgumentList` checks the sibling after `<`. If it's whitespace, it removes it.

But actually, `nextSibling()` returns the next sibling at the same level. For:
```
TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  LT "<"
  WHITE_SPACE "\n    "
  TYPE_PROJECTION
    ...
  COMMA ","
  ...
  GT ">"
```

`node.findChildByType(ElementType.LT)?.nextSibling()` → WHITE_SPACE "\n    "
This is whitespace, so `noWhitespaceExpected` fires and removes it!

This would indeed conflict with wrapping. The `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` should be modified to not remove whitespace that contains newlines.

 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId
115→

content.text:
These are pre-existing warnings. Good.

Now let me think about additional potential issues:

1. **When primary constructor has annotations AND a super type list**: The IndentationRule's `visitClass` builds indent contexts in reverse order. The order is:
   - WHERE (last, if exists)
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST (second to last, if exists)
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR (my addition, if exists and has annotations)
   - The class itself (first)

   Each indent context sets `nextToAstNode` to the prevCodeLeaf of the context start. This creates a chain:
   - Class indent context: from CLASS to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR annotations
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR indent context: from before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR to before SUPER_TYPE_LIST
   
   Wait, this might be wrong. If there's both PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR with annotations AND a SUPER_TYPE_LIST, the indent contexts need to be properly ordered.

   Let me think about the expected output:
   ```kotlin
   data class Foo
       @Inject
       constructor() : Bar {
   ```

   In this case, the super type list (`: Bar`) comes after the constructor. The PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR indent context would cover the annotations through the constructor keyword and parameters. The SUPER_TYPE_LIST indent context would cover the super types.

   But looking at the order in my code, the SUPER_TYPE_LIST context is created BEFORE the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context (because the code is processed top-to-bottom). Since indent contexts are added to a stack, the most recently added context takes priority.

   Actually wait, looking at the `visitClass` code, the contexts are created in order from top to bottom:
   1. WHERE context
   2. SUPER_TYPE_LIST context
   3. PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context (my addition)
   4. CLASS context

   The `nextToAstNode` is chained: each context's start is set as the end of the previous context. So:
   - CLASS context covers from CLASS start to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR annotations
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context covers from PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR annotations to before SUPER_TYPE_LIST
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST context covers the super type list
   
   Wait no, the nextToAstNode chaining works in REVERSE:
   ```
   nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf() // initially covers the whole CLASS
   
   // WHERE sets nextToAstNode to before WHERE
   // SUPER_TYPE_LIST sets nextToAstNode to before SUPER_TYPE_LIST  
   // PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR sets nextToAstNode to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
   
   // CLASS context covers from CLASS to nextToAstNode (before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
   ```

   Actually, the order matters. Since WHERE is checked first, then SUPER_TYPE_LIST, then PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR:
   
   Initially: `nextToAstNode = CLASS.lastChildLeaf()`
   
   After WHERE: `nextToAstNode = WHERE.start.prevCodeLeaf()` (if WHERE exists)
   After SUPER_TYPE_LIST: `nextToAstNode = SUPER_TYPE_LIST.start.prevCodeLeaf()` (if SUPER_TYPE_LIST exists)
   After PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR: `nextToAstNode = PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR.start.prevCodeLeaf()` (if PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR has annotations)
   
   Then CLASS context: from CLASS to nextToAstNode

   The indent contexts on the stack would be (last added = top of stack):
   - CLASS context (childIndent = "")
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context (childIndent = indent)
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST context (childIndent = indent)
   - WHERE context (childIndent = indent)

   Wait, but this means the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context is inside the CLASS context, and the SUPER_TYPE_LIST context is inside the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context. That can't be right if the SUPER_TYPE_LIST is AFTER the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR in the code.

   Let me think about this more carefully. The `startIndentContext` uses `fromAstNode` and `toAstNode`. The `fromAstNode` is the start of the context, and `toAstNode` is the end.

   For SUPER_TYPE_LIST:
   - `fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` → something before the SUPER_TYPE_LIST
   - `toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf()` → the last child of SUPER_TYPE_LIST
   - `nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(...).prevCodeLeaf()` → the code leaf before the SUPER_TYPE_LIST start

   For PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR (my code):
   - `fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces()` → something before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
   - `toAstNode = primaryConstructor.lastChildLeafOrSelf()` → the last child of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
   - `nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(...).prevCodeLeaf()` → the code leaf before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR start

   But the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR ends BEFORE the SUPER_TYPE_LIST starts (in the AST, SUPER_TYPE_LIST is a sibling of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR, coming after it in the CLASS children). So:

   CLASS:
   - ... CLASS_KEYWORD, IDENTIFIER, 
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR (ends at ")")
   - COLON ":"  
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST (starts with super types)
   - ...

   The PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context covers from before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR to its last child (")" of value parameter list).
   The SUPER_TYPE_LIST context covers from before SUPER_TYPE_LIST to its last child.
   The CLASS context covers from CLASS to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR.

   These contexts are non-overlapping and properly ordered! 

   But wait, I need to make sure the `nextToAstNode` chaining is correct. After SUPER_TYPE_LIST is processed, `nextToAstNode` points to before the SUPER_TYPE_LIST. Then when I process PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR, I set `nextToAstNode` to before the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR. Then the CLASS context covers from CLASS to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR.

   But the SUPER_TYPE_LIST context's `toAstNode` is the last child of SUPER_TYPE_LIST. The PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context's `toAstNode` is the last child of PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR. These don't overlap. The gap between them (the COLON and whitespace) is not covered by any indent context... 

   Actually, looking at how the existing code handles it without my changes:
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST context covers from before SUPER_TYPE_LIST to its end
   - CLASS context covers from CLASS to before SUPER_TYPE_LIST

   The gap includes the COLON and whitespace between IDENTIFIER/TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST and SUPER_TYPE_LIST. This gap is covered by the CLASS context (since CLASS context's `toAstNode` is `nextToAstNode`, which is before SUPER_TYPE_LIST).

   With my changes:
   - SUPER_TYPE_LIST context covers from before SUPER_TYPE_LIST to its end
   - PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context covers from before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR to its end
   - CLASS context covers from CLASS to before PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR

   The gap between PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR's end and SUPER_TYPE_LIST's start (the COLON and whitespace) is NOT covered by any indent context. This means the COLON's indentation would fall back to the last indent context on the stack that was activated.

   Hmm, this might cause issues. But actually, the `afterVisitChildNodes` method removes indent contexts when their `toASTNode` is reached. So after the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR context ends, the CLASS context is active again (with childIndent = ""). Then the SUPER_TYPE_LIST context starts. The COLON between them would be indented according to the CLASS context, which has childIndent = "". This is correct - the COLON should be at the same indent as the class.

   Wait, actually, looking at this more carefully, the indent contexts are popped from the stack when `afterVisitChildNodes` is called. The ORDER in which they're popped depends on the `toASTNode`. Let me not worry about this too much since all existing tests pass.

2. **Edge case: class with no super types but constructor annotations**

Let me consider another edge case. Looking at the issue's `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` changes: I modified `visitInsideTypeArgumentList` to not remove whitespace with newlines. This should be safe because:
- Wrapping rules (AnnotationRule, WrappingRule) are responsible for adding newlines inside type argument lists when needed
- The TypeArgumentListSpacingRule should only remove SPACE whitespace (not newlines)
- After wrapping, the IndentationRule fixes the indentation

 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→        visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()) {
150→            // In ktlint_official code style, annotations before a class constructor should be wrapped in same way as
151→            // other annotations. Each annotation should be on a separate line.
152→            if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()) {
153→                // Force the first annotation to be on a separate line from the class keyword/identifier
154→                //   data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2 constructor(...)
155→                // should be formatted as:
156→                //   data class Foo
157→                //       @Bar1
158→                //       @Bar2
159→                //       constructor(...)
160→                emit(
161→                    node.startOffset,
162→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
163→                    true,
164→                )
165→                if (autoCorrect) {
166→                    node
167→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
168→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
169→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
170→                        )
171→                }
172→            }
173→            if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
174→                // Each annotation should be on its own line
175→                emit(
176→                    node.startOffset,
177→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
184→                }
185→            }
186→            if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() &&
187→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry() &&

190→                // Force the annotated construct (constructor keyword) to be on a separate line from the last annotation.
191→                // Only when the last annotation is NOT preceded by another annotation on the same line (because that

193→                emit(
194→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
195→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
196→                    true,
197→                )
198→                if (autoCorrect) {
199→                    node
200→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
201→                        .nextCodeLeaf()

203→                }
204→            }
205→        }

207→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
208→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {

211→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
212→                emit(
213→                    node.startOffset,
214→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
215→                    true,
216→                )
217→                if (autoCorrect) {
218→                    node
219→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
220→                        .nextLeaf()

222→                }
223→            }
224→
225→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
226→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
227→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
228→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
229→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()

235→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
236→                emit(

301→            if (autoCorrect) {
302→                node
303→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
304→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
305→            }
306→        }
307→    }
308→
309→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
310→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
311→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
312→            ?.treeParent
313→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
314→            ?.treeParent
315→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
316→            ?.treeParent
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
319→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
320→        return siblings(forward = false)
321→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
322→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
323→    }
324→
325→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
326→        node: ASTNode,
327→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
328→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
329→    ) {
330→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
331→            emit(
332→                node.startOffset,
333→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
334→                true,
335→            )
336→            if (autoCorrect) {
337→                node
338→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
339→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
340→            }
341→        }
342→
343→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
344→            emit(
345→                node.startOffset,
346→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
347→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
348→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
349→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
350→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
351→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
352→            )
353→            if (autoCorrect) {
354→                node
355→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
356→                    .nextLeaf()
357→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
358→                    ?.let {
359→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
360→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
361→                        } else {
362→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
363→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
364→                            )
365→                        }
366→                    }
367→            }
368→        }
369→    }
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
372→
373→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
374→        psi
375→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
376→            ?.useSiteTarget
377→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
380→
381→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
382→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
383→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
386→        this ==
387→            treeParent
388→                .children()
389→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
390→
391→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
392→        this ==
393→            treeParent
394→                .children()
395→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
396→
397→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
398→        siblings(forward = false)
399→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
400→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
401→
402→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
403→        siblings()
404→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
405→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
406→
407→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
408→        siblings(forward = false)
409→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
410→
411→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
412→        siblings(forward = false)
413→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
414→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
415→
416→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
417→
418→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
419→        siblings(forward = true)
420→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
421→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
422→
423→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
424→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
425→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
426→            .leaves(forward = true)
427→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
428→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
429→    }
430→
431→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
432→        val astNode =
433→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
434→                treeParent
435→            } else {
436→                this
437→            }
438→        return checkNotNull(
439→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
440→        )
441→    }
442→
443→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
444→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
445→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
446→        } else {
447→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
448→        }
449→
450→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor(): Boolean =
451→        treeParent?.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST &&
452→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
453→
454→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier(): Boolean {
455→        // Check if the annotation is on the same line as the class identifier/keyword that precedes the primary
456→        // constructor
457→        val primaryConstructor = treeParent.treeParent
458→        return primaryConstructor
459→            .treePrev
460→            ?.let { prevNode ->
461→                !prevNode.textContains('\n')
462→            }
463→            ?: false
464→    }
465→
466→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
467→        node: ASTNode,
468→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
469→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
470→    ) {
471→        node
472→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
473→            .nextCodeLeaf()
474→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
475→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
476→
477→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
478→                    emit(
479→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
480→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
481→                        true,
482→                    )
483→                    if (autoCorrect) {
484→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
485→                    }
486→                }
487→            }
488→    }
489→
490→    private fun visitAnnotation(
491→        node: ASTNode,
492→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
493→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
494→    ) {
495→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
496→
497→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
498→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
499→        ) {
500→            emit(
501→                node.startOffset,
502→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
503→                true,
504→            )
505→            if (autoCorrect) {
506→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
507→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
508→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
509→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
510→                }
511→            }
512→        }
513→    }
514→
515→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
516→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
517→            modifierListRoot
518→                .treeParent
519→                .treePrev
520→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
521→        val indentWithoutNewline =
522→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
523→                ?.text
524→                .orEmpty()
525→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
526→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
527→    }
528→}
529→
530→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
531→

content.text:
Now let me do a final review of my changes:

### Summary of Changes

**1. AnnotationRule.kt** (Primary change):
- Added import for `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR` and `CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`
- Added `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` to `usesEditorConfigProperties`
- Added new block in `visitAnnotationEntry` for `ktlint_official` + PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR:
  - Forces first annotation to a new line from the class identifier
  - Forces each annotation preceded by another on the same line to its own line  
  - Forces last annotation to be on separate line from the annotated construct (when not already handled by the "multiple annotations" check)
- Added helper methods: `isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()` and `isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()`

**2. IndentationRule.kt** (Indentation support):
- Added import for `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`
- Added handling in `visitClass` for PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR with annotations in `ktlint_official` style
- Creates an indent context for the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR when it has annotations on separate lines

**3. TypeArgumentListSpacingRule.kt** (Compatibility):
- Added import for `isWhiteSpaceWithNewline`
- Modified `visitInsideTypeArgumentList` to skip whitespace with newlines (to not conflict with annotation wrapping inside type argument lists)

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
When using `ktlint_official` code style, annotations before a class constructor should be wrapped to separate lines, similar to how other annotations are handled. Additionally, the `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule` needed to be updated to not conflict with annotation wrapping in type argument lists.

### Files Modified

#### 1. `AnnotationRule.kt` - Main wrapping logic for constructor annotations
- **Added imports**: `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`, `CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`
- **Added `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY`** to `usesEditorConfigProperties` set
- **Added constructor annotation wrapping logic** in `visitAnnotationEntry`:
  - When `codeStyle == ktlint_official` and annotation is part of a `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`:
    - First annotation on same line as class identifier → forces newline between class identifier and annotation
    - Annotation preceded by another annotation on same line → forces each to its own line
    - Last/single annotation on same line as constructor keyword (not preceded by other annotations on same line) → forces newline before constructor
- **Added helper methods**:
  - `isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()` - checks if annotation entry is in a PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR's MODIFIER_LIST
  - `isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()` - checks if the annotation is on the same line as the class identifier/keyword

#### 2. `IndentationRule.kt` - Proper indentation for wrapped constructor annotations
- **Added import**: `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`
- **Added PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR handling** in `visitClass` method:
  - When `ktlint_official` style is used and the PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR has annotations on a separate line from the class identifier, creates an indent context that indents the constructor annotations and keyword one level from the class declaration

#### 3. `TypeArgumentListSpacingRule.kt` - Compatibility with annotation wrapping
- **Added import**: `isWhiteSpaceWithNewline`
- **Modified `visitInsideTypeArgumentList`**: Skip whitespace nodes that contain newlines when checking for unexpected whitespace after `<` and before `>`. This prevents the rule from removing newlines that were inserted by the AnnotationRule or WrappingRule when wrapping annotations on type projections.

 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}

109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId
