 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 14→            """

 16→                @JvmField var foo: String
 17→                @Test fun bar() {}
 18→            }
 19→            """.trimIndent()
 20→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 21→    }
 22→
 23→    @Test
 24→    fun `Given a single annotation on line above the annotated construct`() {
 25→        val code =

 35→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()

 40→        val code =

 62→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /* some comment */ var foo: String
 63→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /* some comment */ fun bar() {}
 64→            }
 65→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ class FooBar3 {
 66→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ var foo: String
 67→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ fun bar() {}

 69→            """.trimIndent()
 70→        val formattedCode =

 77→                fun bar() {}

186→
187→        when {

202→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
203→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
204→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->

565→        node

585→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),

587→            }

633→        ) {

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)

112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }

128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)

145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed

151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()

161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(

180→                if (autoCorrect) {

286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }

310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER

313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()

318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null

321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)

325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }

336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}

574→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
575→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
576→                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
577→                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
578→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
579→            }

586→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
587→            }
588→

590→        startIndentContext(

 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→        visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()) {
150→            // In ktlint_official code style, annotations before a class constructor should be wrapped in same way as
151→            // other annotations. Each annotation should be on a separate line.
152→            if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() && node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
153→                // Force the annotated construct (constructor keyword) to be on a separate line from the last annotation
154→                emit(
155→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
156→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
157→                    true,
158→                )
159→                if (autoCorrect) {
160→                    node
161→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
162→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
163→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
164→                }
165→            }
166→            if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
167→                // Each annotation should be on its own line
168→                emit(
169→                    node.startOffset,
170→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
171→                    true,
172→                )
173→                if (autoCorrect) {
174→                    node
175→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
177→                }
178→            }
179→            if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()) {
180→                // Force the first annotation to be on a separate line from the class keyword/identifier
181→                val prevLeaf = node.treeParent.treeParent.treePrev
182→                if (prevLeaf != null) {
183→                    emit(
184→                        node.startOffset,
185→                        "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
186→                        true,
187→                    )
188→                    if (autoCorrect) {
189→                        node
190→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
191→                            .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
192→                                getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
193→                            )
194→                    }
195→                }
196→            }
197→        }
198→
199→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
200→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
201→                // Code below is disallowed
202→                //   @Foo1
203→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
204→                emit(
205→                    node.startOffset,
206→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
207→                    true,
208→                )
209→                if (autoCorrect) {
210→                    node
211→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
212→                        .nextLeaf()
213→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
214→                }
215→            }
216→
217→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
218→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
219→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
220→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
221→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
222→            ) {
223→                // Code below is disallowed
224→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
225→                // But following is allowed:
226→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
227→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
228→                emit(
229→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
230→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
231→                    true,
232→                )
233→                if (autoCorrect) {
234→                    node
235→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
236→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
237→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
238→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
239→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
240→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
241→                                },
242→                        )
243→                }
244→            }
245→
246→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
247→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
248→                ?.let { startOfList ->
249→                    // Code below is disallowed
250→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
251→                    // But following is allowed:
252→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
253→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
254→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
255→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
256→                    ) {
257→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
258→                        if (autoCorrect) {
259→                            startOfList
260→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
261→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
262→                                )
263→                        }
264→                    }
265→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
266→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
267→                    ) {
268→                        node
269→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
270→                            .treeParent
271→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
272→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
273→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
274→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
275→                                if (autoCorrect) {
276→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
277→                                }
278→                            }
279→                    }
280→                }
281→        }
282→
283→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
284→            // Code below is disallowed
285→            //   @Foo1
286→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
287→            //   fun foo() {}
288→            emit(
289→                node.startOffset,
290→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
291→                true,
292→            )
293→            if (autoCorrect) {
294→                node
295→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
296→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
297→            }
298→        }
299→    }
300→
301→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
302→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
303→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
304→            ?.treeParent
305→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
306→            ?.treeParent
307→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
308→            ?.treeParent
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
311→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
312→        return siblings(forward = false)
313→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
314→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
315→    }
316→
317→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
318→        node: ASTNode,
319→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
320→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
321→    ) {
322→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
323→            emit(
324→                node.startOffset,
325→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
326→                true,
327→            )
328→            if (autoCorrect) {
329→                node
330→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
331→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
332→            }
333→        }
334→
335→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
336→            emit(
337→                node.startOffset,
338→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
339→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
340→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
341→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
342→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
343→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
344→            )
345→            if (autoCorrect) {
346→                node
347→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
348→                    .nextLeaf()
349→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
350→                    ?.let {
351→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
352→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
353→                        } else {
354→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
355→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
356→                            )
357→                        }
358→                    }
359→            }
360→        }
361→    }
362→
363→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
364→
365→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
366→        psi
367→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
368→            ?.useSiteTarget
369→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
372→
373→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
374→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
375→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
376→
377→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
378→        this ==
379→            treeParent
380→                .children()
381→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
382→
383→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
384→        this ==
385→            treeParent
386→                .children()
387→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
388→
389→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
390→        siblings(forward = false)
391→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
392→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
393→
394→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
395→        siblings()
396→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
397→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
398→
399→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
400→        siblings(forward = false)
401→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
402→
403→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
404→        siblings(forward = false)
405→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
406→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
407→
408→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
409→
410→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
411→        siblings(forward = true)
412→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
413→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
414→
415→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
416→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
417→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
418→            .leaves(forward = true)
419→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
420→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
421→    }
422→
423→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
424→        val astNode =
425→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
426→                treeParent
427→            } else {
428→                this
429→            }
430→        return checkNotNull(
431→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
432→        )
433→    }
434→
435→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
436→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
437→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
438→        } else {
439→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
440→        }
441→
442→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor(): Boolean =
443→        treeParent?.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST &&
444→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
445→
446→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier(): Boolean {
447→        // Check if the annotation is on the same line as the class identifier/keyword that precedes the primary
448→        // constructor
449→        val primaryConstructor = treeParent.treeParent
450→        return primaryConstructor
451→            .treePrev
452→            ?.let { prevNode ->
453→                !prevNode.textContains('\n')
454→            }
455→            ?: false
456→    }
457→
458→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
459→        node: ASTNode,
460→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
461→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
462→    ) {
463→        node
464→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
465→            .nextCodeLeaf()
466→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
467→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
468→
469→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
470→                    emit(
471→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
472→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
473→                        true,
474→                    )
475→                    if (autoCorrect) {
476→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
477→                    }
478→                }
479→            }
480→    }
481→
482→    private fun visitAnnotation(
483→        node: ASTNode,
484→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
486→    ) {
487→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
488→
489→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
490→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
491→        ) {
492→            emit(
493→                node.startOffset,
494→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
495→                true,
496→            )
497→            if (autoCorrect) {
498→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
499→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
500→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
501→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
502→                }
503→            }
504→        }
505→    }
506→
507→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
508→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
509→            modifierListRoot
510→                .treeParent
511→                .treePrev
512→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
513→        val indentWithoutNewline =
514→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
515→                ?.text
516→                .orEmpty()
517→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
518→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
519→    }
520→}
521→
522→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 15→/**

 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}

110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }

 48→/**
 49→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 50→ *
 51→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 52→ *
 53→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 54→ *
 55→ *    ```
 56→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 57→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 58→ *    ```
 59→ *
 60→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 61→ *
 62→ *    ```
 63→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 64→ *    var x: String
 65→ *    ```
 66→ *
 67→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 68→ *
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 71→ *    ```
 72→ *
 73→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 74→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 75→ *
 76→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 77→ */
 78→public class AnnotationRule :
 79→    StandardRule(
 80→        id = "annotation",
 81→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 82→            setOf(
 83→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 84→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 86→            ),
 87→        visitorModifiers =
 88→            setOf(
 89→                RunAfterRule(
 90→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 91→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 92→                ),
 93→            ),
 94→    ) {
 95→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 96→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
107→        node: ASTNode,
108→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→    ) {
111→        when (node.elementType) {
112→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
113→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
114→            }
115→            ANNOTATION -> {
116→                // Annotation array
117→                //     @[...]
118→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
121→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
122→            }
123→        }
124→    }
125→
126→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
127→        node: ASTNode,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
130→    ) {
131→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
132→
133→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
134→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
135→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
136→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
137→        ) {
138→            // Disallow:
139→            //     @Foo class Bar
140→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
141→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
142→            //     fun foo(
143→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
144→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
145→            //     ) {}
146→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
147→        }
148→
149→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfPrimaryConstructor()) {
150→            // In ktlint_official code style, annotations before a class constructor should be wrapped in same way as
151→            // other annotations. Each annotation should be on a separate line.
152→            if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsClassIdentifier()) {
153→                // Force the first annotation to be on a separate line from the class keyword/identifier
154→                //   data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2 constructor(...)
155→                // should be formatted as:
156→                //   data class Foo
157→                //       @Bar1
158→                //       @Bar2
159→                //       constructor(...)
160→                emit(
161→                    node.startOffset,
162→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
163→                    true,
164→                )
165→                if (autoCorrect) {
166→                    node
167→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
168→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
169→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
170→                        )
171→                }
172→            }
173→            if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
174→                // Each annotation should be on its own line
175→                emit(
176→                    node.startOffset,
177→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
184→                }
185→            }
186→            if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() &&
187→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry() &&
188→                !node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()
189→            ) {
190→                // Force the annotated construct (constructor keyword) to be on a separate line from the last annotation.
191→                // Only when the last annotation is NOT preceded by another annotation on the same line (because that
192→                // case is already handled by the "Multiple annotations" check below).
193→                emit(
194→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
195→                    "Annotation before \"constructor\" should be placed on a separate line",
196→                    true,
197→                )
198→                if (autoCorrect) {
199→                    node
200→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
201→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
202→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
203→                }
204→            }
205→        }
206→
207→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
208→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
209→                // Code below is disallowed
210→                //   @Foo1
211→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
212→                emit(
213→                    node.startOffset,
214→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
215→                    true,
216→                )
217→                if (autoCorrect) {
218→                    node
219→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
220→                        .nextLeaf()
221→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
222→                }
223→            }
224→
225→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
226→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
227→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
228→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
229→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
230→            ) {
231→                // Code below is disallowed
232→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
233→                // But following is allowed:
234→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
235→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
236→                emit(
237→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
238→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
239→                    true,
240→                )
241→                if (autoCorrect) {
242→                    node
243→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
244→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
245→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
246→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
247→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
248→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
249→                                },
250→                        )
251→                }
252→            }
253→
254→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
255→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
256→                ?.let { startOfList ->
257→                    // Code below is disallowed
258→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
259→                    // But following is allowed:
260→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
261→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
262→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
263→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
264→                    ) {
265→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
266→                        if (autoCorrect) {
267→                            startOfList
268→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
269→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
270→                                )
271→                        }
272→                    }
273→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
274→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
275→                    ) {
276→                        node
277→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
278→                            .treeParent
279→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
280→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
281→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
282→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
283→                                if (autoCorrect) {
284→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
285→                                }
286→                            }
287→                    }
288→                }
289→        }
290→
291→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
292→            // Code below is disallowed
293→            //   @Foo1
294→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
295→            //   fun foo() {}
296→            emit(
297→                node.startOffset,
298→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
299→                true,
300→            )
301→            if (autoCorrect) {
302→                node
303→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
304→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
305→            }
306→        }
307→    }
308→
309→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
310→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
311→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
312→            ?.treeParent
313→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
314→            ?.treeParent
315→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
316→            ?.treeParent
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
319→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
320→        return siblings(forward = false)
321→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
322→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
323→    }
324→
325→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
326→        node: ASTNode,
327→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
328→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
329→    ) {
330→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
331→            emit(
332→                node.startOffset,
333→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
334→                true,
335→            )
336→            if (autoCorrect) {
337→                node
338→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
339→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
340→            }
341→        }
342→
343→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
344→            emit(
345→                node.startOffset,
346→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
347→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
348→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
349→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
350→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
351→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
352→            )
353→            if (autoCorrect) {
354→                node
355→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
356→                    .nextLeaf()
357→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
358→                    ?.let {
359→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
360→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
361→                        } else {
362→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
363→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
364→                            )
365→                        }
366→                    }
367→            }
368→        }
369→    }
